description_long: This is a classical acoustic guitar with a natural wood finish, featuring a rounded body and a flat top with a central sound hole. The front face displays a light-colored spruce or cedar top, contrasted by darker wooden sides and back, likely made of rosewood or mahogany. The neck is long and slender, leading up to a headstock with three tuning pegs on each side, typical of classical guitars. The bridge and fretboard are darker, possibly made from ebony or rosewood, and the guitar is strung with six nylon or classical strings. The back of the guitar is smoothly contoured and finished in a rich, dark wood grain. The overall craftsmanship suggests a traditional, hand-built instrument suitable for street performances or casual play in urban parks.
description: A classical acoustic guitar with a light wood top, dark wood back and sides, and six strings, featuring a traditional rounded body and slotted headstock.
description_view_0: Front view showing the light wood top, sound hole, bridge, and six strings.
description_view_1: Side profile highlighting the guitar's depth, curved body, and neck thickness.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the dark wood back and the full contour of the body and neck.
description_view_3: Opposite side profile showing the same depth and curvature as the other side.
attribute_headstock_design: Slotted headstock with three tuning pegs per side, no visible logo, traditional classical guitar shape.
